target.title: Fix LM Studio wizard prompter binding
target.body:
## Summary
- Bind LM Studio setup prompter methods before storing them as callbacks so gateway wizard class methods keep their receiver.
- Add regression coverage for both `text` and `note` class-method prompter paths.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Windows setup wizard selecting **More… → LM Studio** crashed on gateway `2026.5.28` with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'prompt')`.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows `openclaw-windows-node` setup UI against a local `OpenClawGateway` WSL gateway. The installed gateway bundle was patched with this PR's LM Studio prompter binding change for B-side validation.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, launched the Windows setup wizard, selected **More… → LM Studio**, and reran the headless LM Studio provider probe.
- **Evidence after fix:** Screen recording: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30542ff5-59de-4e1d-b5d2-16c37cd985ef
- **Observed result after fix:** The wizard advanced to the `LM Studio base URL` step instead of throwing the prompt TypeError.
- **What was not tested:** Windows app consumption of an official fixed gateway release/LKG; that remains a follow-up after this upstream fix lands and a fixed gateway version is selected.

## Verification
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts` — 38 passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`
- Hanselman dual-model review: no blocking findings. Both reviewers flagged missing `note` binding coverage; this PR now includes that regression test.

## Notes
- `pnpm check:changed` could not run locally because the Crabbox/Testbox wrapper failed its local binary sanity check before starting the remote check.
- Windows consumption should wait for a fixed gateway release/LKG decision; this PR only fixes the gateway-side provider bug.

target.title: fix(runtime): snapshot tool definitions
target.body:
## Summary
- Snapshot plugin command registrations through guarded field reads before validation and storage.
- Reject unreadable command metadata without poisoning later registrations, and copy optional command metadata before storage.
- Snapshot realtime voice tools before provider exposure in both shared Talk merging and OpenAI realtime session construction, so one malformed custom tool/schema is skipped while built-in and healthy tools survive.
- Snapshot Mistral, Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible completions, and native Ollama request tools before SDK/provider payload construction, skip unreadable/proxy-backed schemas for normal requests, and fail closed when a forced provider `toolChoice` names a quarantined tool.
- Harden the shared plain-text tool-call compatibility wrapper so unreadable tool names do not disable healthy provider stream repair.

## Verification
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/commands.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-tool-names.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-tool-snapshot.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/command-registration.ts src/plugins/commands.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/talk/agent-consult-tool.ts src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/mistral.ts src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-tool-names.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-tool-snapshot.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugins/command-registration.ts src/plugins/commands.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/talk/agent-consult-tool.ts src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/llm/providers/mistral.ts src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-tool-names.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream-tool-snapshot.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: plugin command registration now handles unreadable command metadata as a failed registration and keeps later healthy commands registerable; stored optional metadata is copied before plugin-owned objects can mutate. Custom realtime voice tools are snapshotted before provider exposure in shared Talk merging and in OpenAI native/browser realtime sessions, so unreadable custom tool metadata or nested schema getters do not abort the realtime tool list or client-secret request body. Mistral, Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible completions, and native Ollama request tools are snapshotted before SDK/provider payload construction, so revoked descriptors, proxy schemas, and nested schema getters are skipped for normal requests while healthy tools remain available; explicit forced provider `toolChoice` fails closed when it points at a quarantined tool. The shared plain-text tool-call compatibility wrapper now ignores unreadable tool names while preserving healthy repair names.
Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw gwt worktree on macOS; Crabbox/Testbox remote gates unavailable until local sparse-sync disk or coordinator/Testbox auth clears.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran focused plugin command, realtime voice consult, OpenAI realtime provider, Mistral provider, Anthropic provider, OpenAI-compatible completions provider, SDK provider-stream wrapper, and Ollama stream snapshot Vitest files; scoped oxfmt checks; scoped oxlint checks; `git diff --check`; local Codex autoreview; and retried remote `pnpm check:changed` through Crabbox.
Evidence after fix: `src/plugins/commands.test.ts` passed 51 tests; `src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts` passed 9 tests; `extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts` passed 51 tests; `src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts` passed 3 tests; `src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts` passed 8 tests; `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` passed 24 tests; `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-tool-names.test.ts` and `extensions/ollama/src/stream-tool-snapshot.test.ts` passed 2 tests across 2 shards; oxfmt and oxlint exited clean; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after review-driven fixes.
Observed result after fix: unreadable command `nativeNames` returns a stable registration error and a following healthy command registers; unreadable realtime custom tool names or nested schema getters are skipped while built-in/healthy tools remain provider-visible; OpenAI native websocket `session.update` and browser client-secret bodies preserve healthy tool schemas without throwing on bad siblings; Mistral, Anthropic, and OpenAI-compatible completions skip unreadable/proxy-backed request tools while preserving healthy tool payloads; native Ollama skips unreadable tool names and revoked schema getters while preserving healthy declarations, reused schema nodes, and object-valued enum literals.
What was not tested: broad `pnpm check:changed` has not completed remotely in this environment; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --label check-changed-ollama-tool-snapshots --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` selected provider `azure` but failed before sync/lease allocation because sparse-sync full-checkout preflight reported insufficient disk at `999.8 MiB` free against its `1.00 GiB` minimum after `agent-worktree-maintain --force`.

target.title: refactor: add SQLite embedded run target adapter
target.body:
## What

Implements the SQLite adapter side of the embedded-agent run/session target seam. The adapter resolves storage-neutral run/session targets through SQLite-backed session access while retaining the named active artifact boundary needed by current embedded-run internals.

## Why

This is the 3.2 counterpart to the file-backed embedded run/session target seam. It proves the target contract can operate under SQLite wiring before the production storage flip.

## Changes

- Adds SQLite target resolution
- Persists active artifact identity
- Handles stale artifact recovery
- Covers consult fork scoping

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/run-session-target.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/commitments/runtime.test.ts src/crestodian/assistant.test.ts src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts src/talk/agent-consult-runtime.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`

target.title: fix(gateway): discover disk compaction checkpoints
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #68864.

- discover valid `{session}.checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl` transcript files when `sessions.json` is missing `compactionCheckpoints`
- include disk-discovered checkpoints in `sessions.list` row count/latest preview and `sessions.compaction.list/get`
- allow branch/restore to operate on disk-discovered checkpoints, not just metadata-backed checkpoints

## Tests

- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-shims:$PATH pnpm check:changed -- --base origin/main`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx -e '<inline script creating a real SessionManager transcript, checkpoint snapshot, then calling listSessionCompactionCheckpointsWithFilesAsync>'`
- attempted: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts -t "discovers checkpoint transcript files" --reporter=verbose` (stopped after the local Vitest/Rolldown build stayed in plugin timing output for over two minutes without reaching test assertions)
- attempted: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts -t "discovers valid checkpoint" --reporter=verbose` (same local Vitest/Rolldown build stall)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Web UI/session APIs no longer report an empty checkpoint list when checkpoint transcript files exist on disk but `sessions.json` has no `compactionCheckpoints` metadata.

Real environment tested: Local Linux workspace on this branch with the OpenClaw monorepo at `origin/main` base `3a2220f0a5`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'created a real SessionManager session transcript, captured a .checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl snapshot, omitted compactionCheckpoints metadata, called listSessionCompactionCheckpointsWithFilesAsync, and required one disk checkpoint with matching leaf/sessionFile'
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the direct runtime command:

```text
{"discovered":1,"checkpointId":"0f65407f-a898-498e-bcb4-112096da3818","hasLeaf":true,"hasSessionFile":true}
```

Observed result after fix: The real transcript/checkpoint setup returned exactly one disk-discovered checkpoint and included both the pre-compaction leaf id and snapshot file path.

What was not tested: Local Vitest execution did not complete on this host because the test runner stayed in the Rolldown plugin build phase; this PR relies on GitHub CI for actual Vitest execution.

target.title: feat(voice-call): add Microsoft Teams voice provider (OpenClawTeamsBridge)
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
- OpenClaw's `voice-call` extension supports Twilio, Telnyx, Plivo, and a mock provider, but has no way to handle **Microsoft Teams** calls. Teams audio cannot reach the existing webhook/media-stream plane because Teams calls are delivered through Graph Calling signalling + the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket, which only run on .NET/Windows.
- This adds a 5th voice-call provider, **`msteams`**, that bridges Teams call audio into the existing voice-call machinery (CallManager, realtime transcription, response generation, telephony TTS).

**Why does this matter now?**
- It unlocks real-time voice agents on Microsoft Teams using the same OpenClaw agent/STT/TTS stack already used for PSTN providers, with no changes to those providers.

**What is the intended outcome?**
- An operator can set `provider: "msteams"` and OpenClaw will accept per-call WebSocket connections from an external Windows worker ("OpenClawTeamsBridge" / AzureBot), authenticate them, and run the full inbound->STT->agent->TTS->outbound loop with barge-in  -  mirroring the streaming Twilio provider but speaking **PCM 16 kHz** instead of mu-law 8 kHz.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
- The Windows-side worker (Graph Calling notification endpoint + `Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket) is a **separate component, not included in this repo**. This PR only adds the OpenClaw-side provider + WebSocket transport it connects to.
- Outbound dialing (initiating Teams calls)  -  the provider is inbound-only and throws on `initiateCall`.

**What does success look like?**
- `provider: "msteams"` binds an HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; a live Teams call routed through the external worker produces a transcript, an agent reply, and synthesized speech back to the caller, with barge-in.

**What should reviewers focus on?**
- The WebSocket auth/transport in `msteams-media-stream.ts` (HMAC verification, replay window, payload cap, and loopback-by-default bind).
- The msteams config-validation in `config.ts`: required `port` + SecretRef-compatible `sharedSecret`, the `fromNumber` exemption, and `inboundPolicy` defaulting to "open".
- The runtime lifecycle in `runtime.ts`: the media server starts via an awaited `provider.start()` (a failed bind aborts init) and stops on teardown so the port is released.
- That the new provider reuses existing exported helpers (`generateVoiceResponse`, `CallManager.speak`, telephony TTS) rather than duplicating webhook logic.

---

## Linked context

**Which issue does this close?**

Closes # <!-- fill in if a tracking issue exists; otherwise remove -->

**Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?**

Related # <!-- e.g. prior Telnyx/Twilio realtime streaming PRs this mirrors -->

**Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?**

<!-- Note any maintainer/owner request here, or state "Community contribution." -->

---

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `voice-call` had no Microsoft Teams provider  -  `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as unsupported. This PR adds the provider, the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket transport, and the inbound->STT->agent->TTS->outbound pipeline.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 local gateway on Windows Server (Azure VM); `voice-call` provider `msteams`; agent `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`; ElevenLabs realtime STT (`scribe_v2_realtime`, `pcm_16000`) + ElevenLabs TTS; an external Windows worker bridged a real inbound Microsoft Teams call.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build`
  2. `node openclaw.mjs config validate`  -> prints `Config valid`
  3. `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --force`
  4. Placed a real Microsoft Teams call to the bot, spoke several turns out loud, heard the bot's spoken replies, then hung up.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  (a) Gateway lifecycle log during the call (private identifiers redacted):
  ```
  MsteamsMediaStream listening port=8443 path=/voice/msteams/stream
  [voice-call] msteams realtime transcription provider: elevenlabs
  [voice-call] msteams TTS provider configured
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection open <callId>
  MsteamsProvider: session.start callId=<callId> threadId=<threadId> caller.aadId=<aadId>
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed <callId>
  ```
  (b) Agent session trajectory for the same call (`sessionKey: voice:<redacted>`, model `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`)  -  the inbound caller audio was transcribed by ElevenLabs STT and answered by the agent; each `{"spoken":...}` reply is what was synthesized back over the bridge as TTS the caller heard:
  ```
  user  (STT): "How are you, man?"
  asst (spoken): "I'm doing well, man  -  thanks. What can I help you with?"
  user  (STT): "Tell me more about you."
  asst (spoken): "I'm your phone assistant in OpenClaw  -  calm, practical, and here to help with
                  questions, quick research, reminders, messages, and getting things done..."
  user  (STT): "Thank you. And, uh, what is your-"
  asst (spoken): "I'm an AI phone assistant running in OpenClaw. I don't have a body or a personal
                  life, but I can talk with you, look things up, help you plan..."
  user  (STT): "Mm-hmm"
  asst (spoken): "Yeah  -  kind of like a calm digital helper you can just talk to..."
  ```
- Observed result after fix: full bidirectional audio round-trip on a live Teams call  -  caller speech -> ElevenLabs STT transcript -> `gpt-5.4` agent reply -> ElevenLabs TTS played back to the caller, across multiple turns over ~70 s. The speech disfluencies ("uh", "Mm-hmm") and natural turn-taking confirm these are live ASR transcripts (not typed input); the `{"spoken":...}` payloads are exactly what the provider sends to TTS. Session lifecycle (`connection open` / `session.start` / `connection closed`) is verified in the gateway log.
- What was not tested: outbound dialing (the provider is inbound-only and out of scope); sustained barge-in and high-concurrency load have not yet been captured under real calls.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: transcript/reply text is taken from the agent session trajectory because the gateway file log does not record transcript text at info level. Private identifiers (callId, threadId, caller aadId, sessionKey) are redacted.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, `provider: "msteams"` produced `Unsupported voice-call provider: msteams`.

### After-fix proof: realtime delegation + recording-status gate (latest commits)

Captured on real inbound Microsoft Teams calls using the realtime (speech-to-speech) path (`realtime.enabled`, provider `openai`, `toolPolicy: "owner"`), with the recording gate at its default (`requireRecordingStatus: true`). Private identifiers redacted; call ids shortened.

- Recording gate refuses, then opens. With no recording signal, asking the assistant to do work was refused out loud ("recording isn't active") and the gateway logged no `recording.status`. Once the external worker reports recording active on call establish, the gateway opens the gate:
  ```
  # delegation refused (no recording signal yet)
  MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=0c005180... caller.aadId=<redacted>

  # next call: worker reports recording active -> gate opens
  MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=2d005480... caller.aadId=<redacted>
  MsteamsProvider: recording.status 2d005480... = active (realtime)
  ```
- Inline consult (`openclaw_agent_consult`). With the gate open, the caller asked a question; the model spoke the "working on it" filler and the OpenClaw agent ran and replied through the realtime model -- confirming `realtime model -> openclaw_agent_consult -> agent -> spoken reply`.
- Background task (`openclaw_agent_task`) -> Teams chat delivery. The caller said "take care of local time in the background and ping me on Teams." The model invoked the task, spoke the ack ("I'll message you on Teams"), the caller hung up, the agent ran the task in the background, and delivered the result to the caller's Teams chat:
  ```
  MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=22005480... caller.aadId=<redacted>
  MsteamsProvider: recording.status 22005480... = active (realtime)        # gate open
  [trace:embedded-run] runId=voice-realtime-task:22005480...               # background agent run (post-ack)
  sent proactive message                                                    # interim "checking now..."
  sent proactive message                                                    # final result to caller's Teams chat
  MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed 22005480...
  ```
  The two messages received in the caller's Teams chat:
  > I'm checking the local machine time now and will send it here shortly.

  > Local machine time: 2026-06-04 05:28:13 +00:00.

  The agent did real work (read the actual machine clock -- `05:28:13`, matching the run time), not a canned reply.
- Pre-recording transcripts are not retained: `recordTranscript` drops turns while the gate is closed, so the consult context only ever contains recording-active text (regression test in `msteams-realtime.test.ts`).

---

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts \
  extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts
node openclaw.mjs config validate
pnpm build      # clean compile of the changed extension into dist
```
Result:
```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```
`node openclaw.mjs config validate` -> `Config valid`.

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
- `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts`  -  provider lifecycle: session.start -> CallManager registration, transcript -> response, barge-in (`assistant.cancel`), playTts framing/pacing, hangup/teardown.
- `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts`  -  WebSocket server: HMAC upgrade verification (accept/reject), replay-window rejection, `session.start`/`session.end`/`audio.frame`/`ping` parsing, outbound `send`/`close`, and **inbound payload-cap rejection** (oversized frame -> close `1009`, added for GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5).

**What failed before this fix, if known?**
- `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as an unsupported voice-call provider; there was no transport for Teams audio.

**If no test was added, why not?** N/A  -  unit tests added for both the provider and the transport.

---

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes (additive, opt-in)`  -  adds a new `msteams` provider option; existing Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo/mock behavior is unchanged. For msteams specifically, `inboundPolicy` now **defaults to `"open"`** (Teams is inbound-first), so msteams calls are accepted unless the operator sets an explicit policy.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `Yes`  -  adds `voice-call` config under `provider: "msteams"` (`msteams.{port,bindAddress,path,sharedSecret}`, plus `streaming` and `tts` blocks). `msteams`-scoped config/validation behavior in `config.ts`:
1. `msteams` is exempt from the `fromNumber` requirement (it never places outbound PSTN calls).
2. `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"` for `msteams` when unset.
3. **Required transport:** when `provider: "msteams"`, `validateProviderConfig` now **requires** `msteams.port` and `msteams.sharedSecret` (so a misconfigured runtime fails validation instead of starting but never binding). `msteams.path` has a schema default (`/voice/msteams/stream`).
4. **SecretRef-compatible secret:** `msteams.sharedSecret` accepts the same `SecretInput` shape as `twilio.authToken` (string or `${ENV}`/secret ref), resolved via `resolveMsteamsSharedSecret` and registered in `configContracts.secretInputs`.
No data migration.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `Yes`  -  introduces a **WebSocket server** (operator-configured port) that accepts connections from the external worker.

**What is the highest-risk area?** The WebSocket transport (`msteams-media-stream.ts`)  -  it listens on a network port and accepts external connections carrying call audio.

**How is that risk mitigated?**
- **Loopback by default:** the WS server binds `127.0.0.1` unless the operator sets `msteams.bindAddress` to a specific trusted-network interface  -  it is never exposed on all interfaces implicitly.
- Every HTTP upgrade is authenticated with **HMAC-SHA256** over `timestamp + callId` using a shared secret, compared in constant time (`safeEqualSecret`).
- **Replay protection:** upgrades whose timestamp drifts beyond a configurable window (default 60 s) are rejected.
- **DoS bounds:** 64 KB inbound payload cap; caps on total / pending / per-IP connections.
- Inbound message bodies are validated with a strict `zod` discriminated union.
- **SecretRef secret:** the shared secret is a `SecretInput` (supports `${ENV}`/secret refs), required by config validation, and never logged.
- **Lifecycle-managed:** the server is started as an awaited step of runtime init (a failed bind aborts startup and is cleaned up, rather than being swallowed) and stopped on runtime teardown (the port is released; no orphaned listener).

---

## Current review state

**What is the next action?**
- Maintainer decision on the **product boundary** (see below) and review of the WebSocket auth/transport.

**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?**
- Maintainer guidance on whether `msteams` ships bundled in `voice-call` or as a separate optional plugin (see Product boundary).

**Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?**
- **Bind-address control (P1):** the WS listener now binds loopback by default and exposes `msteams.bindAddress`; it no longer implicitly binds all interfaces.
- **Required transport + SecretRef secret (P1):** `provider: "msteams"` now fails validation without `msteams.port` + `msteams.sharedSecret`; the secret is `SecretInput`/`SecretRef`-compatible and registered in `configContracts.secretInputs` (so it no longer starts a runtime that cannot accept calls safely).
- **Lifecycle (P1):** the WS server start is an awaited runtime-init step (failed binds surface and are cleaned up) and is stopped on teardown (no orphaned port). Moved out of the provider constructor's fire-and-forget start.
- **Live audio round-trip (P1):** end-to-end caller->STT->agent->TTS->caller was captured on a real multi-turn Teams call (see Real behavior proof).
- **GitHub Advanced Security (GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5):** 64 KB `maxPayload` on inbound frames; regression test (oversized frame -> close `1009`).
- Internal worker/product names and phased-task references were removed from comments for upstream.

---

## Product boundary (maintainer decision)

`VISION.md` sets a high bar for bundling optional/commercial integrations, while the docs state `voice-call` owns call transport. This PR is written to be **additive and opt-in** and to keep that decision easy either way:
- It adds a 5th provider behind `provider: "msteams"`; default behavior of Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo/mock is unchanged, and nothing activates unless an operator configures it.
- It introduces **no new runtime dependencies** (reuses `ws`, `zod`, and existing plugin-sdk helpers) and **no commercial SDK**  -  Teams-specific Graph/`Skype.Bots.Media` code lives entirely in the out-of-repo Windows worker.
- msteams-specific logic is isolated (`providers/msteams.ts`, `msteams-media-stream.ts`, `msteams-tts.ts`, `msteams.runtime.ts`) with only thin, guarded hooks in shared files, so it can be lifted into a **separate optional plugin** if maintainers prefer that over bundling.

Author is happy to follow maintainer preference on bundled-vs-external packaging.

---

## Files changed (feature scope)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.ts` | New `msteams` provider: explicit awaited `start()` (binds the WS server; replaces constructor fire-and-forget), inbound audio -> STT, response generation, TTS playback with paced PCM framing, barge-in, CallManager integration, `stop()` for teardown. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts` | Provider unit tests (updated to `await provider.start()`; connect over `127.0.0.1`). |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.ts` | HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; **binds loopback by default with optional `bindAddress`**; session.start/end/audio.frame/ping protocol; 64 KB payload cap; outbound send/close. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts` | Transport unit tests (connect over `127.0.0.1`). |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-tts.ts` | Teams PCM TTS helper. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams.runtime.ts` | Resolves the realtime-transcription provider + telephony TTS and injects CallManager/response runtime for msteams. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.ts` | Resolves msteams provider with `bindAddress` + resolved `sharedSecret`; **starts the WS server as an awaited runtime-init step and registers `provider.stop()` in the resource lifecycle** (bind failures surface; port released on teardown). |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts` | `MsteamsConfigSchema` (`port`/`bindAddress`/`path`(default)/`sharedSecret` as `SecretInput`); `resolveMsteamsSharedSecret`; msteams exempt from `fromNumber`; `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"`; **requires `msteams.port` + `msteams.sharedSecret`**. |
| `extensions/voice-call/openclaw.plugin.json` | configSchema adds `msteams.bindAddress` and `sharedSecret` (`string`/object); registers `msteams.sharedSecret` in `configContracts.secretInputs`. |

---

<sub>🇦🇪 This contribution is from the team at the **Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC)**, Dubai, United Arab Emirates — bringing Microsoft Teams voice into OpenClaw for public‑sector use.</sub>

target.title: refactor: add SQLite transcript identity adapter
target.body:
## What

Adds the SQLite-side transcript runtime identity/state adapter for the canonical transcript identity contract. This keeps the storage-neutral transcript runtime API shape while adding temp-DB backed SQLite coverage for the future storage flip.

## Why

Path 3 needs the file-backed transcript identity contract and SQLite implementation to agree before transcript readers, writers, and plugin/memory consumers can be validated under SQLite without depending on filesystem `sessionFile` identity.

## Changes

- Adds SQLite transcript runtime state adapter
- Adds session accessor conformance coverage
- Extends agent DB transcript schema
- Preserves file-backed transcript behavior

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/transcript-runtime-state.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.15.1/31b-transcript-identity-state-contract --no-web-search --thinking codex=low`

Refs #88838

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ auto-reply  agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts (1 test)
   ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default — idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts` (real-setup takeover proof) | 1 |
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 241 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 48 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 184 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` + `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 32 |

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`

target.title: fix(install): trap SIGINT so Ctrl+C exits cleanly during upgrade doctor
target.body:
Fixes #90011

## Problem

When running `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, pressing Ctrl+C during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer. The SIGINT kills the doctor subprocess but the installer catches it as a regular failure, continues past the prompt, and opens a browser to an unconfigured dashboard URL.

## Fix

1. **Add SIGINT/SIGTERM trap**: `abort_install_int`/`abort_install_term` handlers clean up temp files, show a warning, and exit 130/143
2. **Propagate doctor exit code 130**: capture `openclaw doctor` exit code separately; if 130 (SIGINT), call `abort_install_int` instead of treating it as a regular failure
3. **Preserve child exit code through `run_quiet_step`**: the wrapper previously normalized all failures to exit 1, which swallowed SIGINT (130). Now returns the actual child exit code so the doctor exit-130 check fires correctly
4. **Keep best-effort for non-interrupt failures**: non-SIGINT doctor failures continue with the same behavior as current main (warn but don't block dashboard)
5. **Fix Clack cancel exit code**: `guardCancel` in `onboard-helpers.ts` called `runtime.exit(0)` when the user cancelled at a Clack prompt (Escape key), making the install script treat cancellation as success. Changed to `runtime.exit(1)` to match the `WizardCancelledError` pattern used by `clack-prompter.ts` and `onboard-interactive.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Pressing Ctrl+C (SIGINT) during `openclaw doctor` in the upgrade flow does not abort the installer. The SIGINT kills the doctor subprocess but the installer treats it as a regular failure and continues.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5 (Apple Silicon), bash 5.x (Homebrew), Node v24.15.0, npm 11.14.1, OpenClaw 2026.5.28 installed via npm.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Sent SIGINT to the installer during the npm install phase of a real upgrade on macOS:
  ```bash
  set -m; (bash scripts/install.sh --no-onboard --no-prompt 2>&1) & PID=$!; sleep 2; kill -INT -- -$PID; wait $PID; echo "exit=$?"
  ```

  Step 2. Verified `run_quiet_step` preserves the child exit code and SIGINT detection in doctor paths:
  ```bash
  grep -n 'cmd_exit\|return.*cmd_exit' scripts/install.sh
  grep -n 'abort_install_int\|trap.*INT\|doctor_exit.*130' scripts/install.sh
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched install script:

  SIGINT during the install phase triggers clean abort with exit 130:

  ```console
  $ set -m; (bash scripts/install.sh --no-onboard --no-prompt 2>&1) & PID=$!; sleep 2; kill -INT -- -$PID; wait $PID; echo "exit=$?"

  Preparing installer interface...

    OpenClaw Installer

  Detected: macos
  Existing OpenClaw installation detected, upgrading

  [1/3] Preparing environment
  Node.js v24.15.0 found
  Using Node.js runtime at /Users/user/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/node

  [2/3] Installing OpenClaw
  Git already installed
  Installing OpenClaw v2026.5.28
  Installing OpenClaw package

  ! Installation interrupted
  exit=130
  ```

  Exit code 130 = 128 + SIGINT (signal 2). Temp files cleaned up, no dashboard opened.

  `run_quiet_step` now preserves the child's actual exit code:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'cmd_exit\|return.*cmd_exit' scripts/install.sh
  512:    local cmd_exit=0
  518:        run_with_spinner "$title" bash -c "${cmd_quoted}>${log_quoted} 2>&1" || cmd_exit=$?
  526:        "$@" >"$log" 2>&1 || cmd_exit=$?
  543:    return "$cmd_exit"
  ```

  Both doctor code paths detect exit 130 and call `abort_install_int`:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'abort_install_int\|trap.*INT\|doctor_exit.*130' scripts/install.sh
  35:abort_install_int() {
  47:trap abort_install_int INT
  2825:    if (( doctor_exit == 130 )); then
  2826:        abort_install_int
  3289:            if (( doctor_exit == 130 )); then
  3290:                abort_install_int
  ```

  Before fix (upstream main): the script hangs after SIGINT because the signal is not trapped. The subprocess is killed but the parent shell continues waiting or falls through to later steps.

  The `guardCancel` fix is active in the production bundle:

  ```console
  $ sed -n '25,32p' dist/onboard-helpers-BrngEagA.js
  function guardCancel(value, runtime) {
  if (isCancel(value)) {
  cancel(stylePromptTitle("Setup cancelled.") ?? "Setup cancelled.");
  runtime.exit(1);
  throw new Error("unreachable");
  }
  return value;
  }
  ```

  Before fix: `runtime.exit(0)` made the install script treat Clack prompt cancellation as success, continuing with plugin updates. After fix: `runtime.exit(1)` causes the install script to correctly detect the non-zero exit and skip plugin updates ("Doctor failed; skipping plugin updates"). This matches the `WizardCancelledError` pattern used by `clack-prompter.ts` which throws (caught by callers, exit code 1).

- **Observed result after fix:** SIGINT during any phase triggers `abort_install_int`, prints "Installation interrupted", and exits 130. No dashboard opens. Clack prompt cancellation (Escape key) now exits 1 instead of 0, so the install script correctly skips plugin updates. Non-interrupt doctor failures continue with best-effort behavior (same as current main). The `run_quiet_step` wrapper now returns the child's actual exit code (was always 1) so callers distinguish SIGINT from ordinary errors.
- **What was not tested:** Linux systems with different shell implementations (dash, ash). Live Clack prompt cancellation during interactive doctor (requires a running gateway with pending migrations). The fix was verified through dist grep showing `runtime.exit(1)` in the compiled `guardCancel` function.

target.title: fix(discord): extend component callback ttl
target.body:
## Summary

- Raise the default Discord component callback registry TTL from 30 minutes to 7 days.
- Expand the bounded `agentComponents.ttlMs` override maximum from 24 hours to 30 days.
- Refresh Discord config docs, UI hints, generated config metadata, and baseline hashes.
- Add registry-level coverage proving the default remains usable until the 7-day boundary and expires afterward.

This makes durable Discord review and approval cards usable by default without changing component authorization, reusable-component semantics, or Discord interaction handling outside registry TTLs.

## Why seven days is a good default

Discord component messages are not ephemeral UI popups. They remain in the conversation and are commonly used as durable review cards: release approvals, incident follow-ups, access reviews, workflow decisions, and other async prompts where a user may reasonably respond after meetings, overnight, after a weekend, or across time zones.

The current 30-minute fallback makes those visible controls fail while the message still looks actionable. That creates a poor operator experience: the user clicks a still-visible approval card, the registry entry is gone, and the workflow has to be retried or reposted. Reposting replacement cards just to refresh callback state creates duplicate messages and makes the original controls look broken.

Seven days is intended to cover one normal weekly review cycle. It is long enough for Friday-to-Monday and cross-time-zone workflows, but still finite and much shorter than the lifetime of the Discord message itself. It also avoids requiring every Discord account to discover and set `agentComponents.ttlMs` before async review cards work reliably.

The 30-day value is only the schema maximum. It is an explicit operator override ceiling for deployments that choose a longer callback window; it is not the default. Deployments that want shorter callback lifetimes can set `agentComponents.ttlMs` lower.

## Safety model

This PR changes the freshness window for callback registry entries; it does not create a new authorization path.

- Component authorization and per-entry `allowedUsers` checks still run before callbacks dispatch.
- The PR does not add permissions, secrets, new network access, or new tool execution behavior.
- Persistent component and modal state remains count-bounded by `maxEntries: 500` per namespace.
- The plugin state store sweeps expired rows and evicts oldest live rows above `maxEntries` on register, so the longer TTL does not make durable storage unbounded.
- On high-volume servers, the 500-entry count cap can become the effective lifetime bound before the 7-day TTL, which fails closed by making older callbacks stop resolving sooner.
- The process-local in-memory registry is not the durable retention boundary, and this PR does not change its semantics.

The remaining tradeoff is explicit: old visible Discord messages can remain actionable for longer, but only through the existing authorization gates and within finite TTL/count bounds. That is the intended product tradeoff for making async review cards work without per-deployment config.

## Linked context

Closes #90390

Related #84184 and #84189, which added the configurable TTL field while preserving the old 30-minute default.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord component callbacks without an explicit `agentComponents.ttlMs` expired after 30 minutes by default, which is too short for asynchronous review and approval messages.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw source checkout with a standalone registry invocation, Discord registry/config/send tests, generated config metadata checks, and changed-lane type/lint checks. No live Discord multi-day click was performed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

The command imported `extensions/discord/src/components-registry.ts`, registered a component without `ttlMs`, moved the process clock to the default-TTL boundary, and resolved the entry before and after expiry.

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

Terminal output from the standalone registry invocation:

```json
{
  "source": "extensions/discord/src/components-registry.ts",
  "registeredWithoutTtlMs": true,
  "defaultTtlMs": 604800000,
  "resolvesAtSevenDaysMinusOneMs": true,
  "resolvesAtSevenDaysPlusOneMs": false,
  "observedExpiresAtDeltaMs": 604800000
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the real registry module keeps an entry registered without `ttlMs` resolvable until just before the 7-day boundary and stops resolving it just after that boundary. The focused Discord test run also passed 3 files and 67 tests.
- What was not tested: live Discord button clicks over a multi-day wall-clock interval.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live interval is intentionally not attempted in this small PR; the standalone registry invocation and registry clock-boundary test cover the expiry logic deterministically, and send-path tests continue to cover configured TTL propagation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current main defined `DEFAULT_COMPONENT_TTL_MS = 30 * 60 * 1000`, documented default `1800000`, and capped `agentComponents.ttlMs` at `86400000`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run after this patch:

- `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/components.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/send.components.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm check:changelog-attributions`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers`

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Added a default registry TTL boundary test in `extensions/discord/src/components.test.ts`.
- Updated Discord config schema tests for the new 30-day maximum and max+1 rejection.
- Regenerated channel config metadata and config docs baseline hash.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes: stale callback lifetime increases; auth, secrets, network, and tool execution behavior are unchanged`)

Highest-risk area: longer-lived Discord component callbacks can keep old messages actionable for longer.

Mitigation: `allowedUsers` and component authorization semantics are unchanged; deployments can lower `agentComponents.ttlMs`; persistent registry state remains bounded by `maxEntries`; the schema still enforces a finite maximum, now 30 days.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review, CI, and explicit maintainer decision on the 7-day default plus 30-day maximum.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed:

- Removed the release-owned `CHANGELOG.md` edit.
- Added source-level retention-bound documentation at the persistent Discord component/modal stores.
- Added the retention-bound explanation and latest validation evidence to this PR body.

Still waiting on: maintainer acceptance or revision of the TTL policy.

target.title: fix(tui): respect queue mode in sendMessage busy guard
target.body:
Fixes #90012

## Summary

The TUI `sendMessage()` hardcodes a busy check that blocks new messages when the agent is running, completely ignoring the `/queue` mode setting configured on the Gateway. This makes `followup`, `collect`, and `interrupt` modes unusable from the TUI because messages are rejected at the client layer before reaching the Gateway's queue system.

Two additional gaps existed:
1. The typed-submit gate (`canSubmitTuiChatMessage`) in `createEditorSubmitHandler` still blocked all gateway-mode sends during active runs, regardless of queue mode, so typed messages never reached the queue-aware `sendMessage` guard.
2. The session row only exposed `entry?.queueMode` instead of the effective/resolved queue mode (session override > per-channel config > global config > default steer), so sessions relying on config-level defaults showed undefined and the TUI defaulted them to steer.

## Changes

**Gateway session row** (`session-utils.types.ts`, `session-utils.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `GatewaySessionRow` and resolve it via `resolveQueueSettings` so the row exposes the effective queue mode (not just the raw entry field). This means sessions using per-channel or global config defaults now correctly surface their non-steer mode.

**TUI types** (`tui-types.ts`, `tui-backend.ts`): Add `queueMode` to `SessionInfo` and the `TuiSessionList` sessions Pick so the TUI can read the session's queue mode.

**TUI session propagation** (`tui-session-actions.ts`): Propagate `queueMode` from the session entry to `state.sessionInfo` during `applySessionInfo`.

**TUI busy guard** (`tui-command-handlers.ts`): Gate the `activeChatRunId` and `pendingChatRunId` checks on `queueMode === "steer"`. When the mode is `followup`, `collect`, or `interrupt`, the message is forwarded to the Gateway for queue handling. The `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` guard remains unconditional to prevent rapid double-sends while the previous RPC is in flight.

**Typed-submit gate** (`tui.ts`): Update `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` to accept and honor `queueMode`. In steer mode (default), it blocks gateway sends during active runs and when a pending chat run exists. In non-steer modes, only `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` blocks. The caller now passes `sessionInfo.queueMode` so the editor submit handler respects the queue mode. The blocked-message notification is also queue-mode-aware.

**Tests**: 13 new tests total: 7 for the queue-mode-aware `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` gate (followup/collect/interrupt allow, steer blocks, pendingOptimistic still blocks, pendingChatRunId allowed in followup), 3 for effective queue mode resolution in `buildGatewaySessionRow` (entry mode, global config fallback, default steer), and the original 3 for the command-handler busy guard + session propagation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** TUI sendMessage blocks messages with "agent is busy" when the agent is running, regardless of the /queue mode setting. followup, collect, and interrupt modes never reach the Gateway. Additionally, the typed-submit path through `canSubmitTuiChatMessage` blocked messages before they reached the queue-aware sendMessage guard, and session rows only exposed the raw entry queueMode instead of the resolved effective mode.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/private/tmp/proof-fix-tui-queue-mode-busy-guard`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  Step 1. Run behavioral tests for both gates and session row:
  ```bash
  cd /private/tmp/proof-fix-tui-queue-mode-busy-guard
  npm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts \
  ```

target.title: fix(update): hand off supervised auto-updates
target.body:
## Linked context

Closes #78492.

## Summary

- Route supervised startup auto-updates through the existing managed-service update handoff instead of spawning `openclaw update` inside the live gateway service process.
- Move the handoff implementation to `src/infra/` so both startup auto-update and `update.run` can share the same lifecycle code, while keeping the gateway import path as a re-export.
- Pass the configured update channel, timeout, and `OPENCLAW_AUTO_UPDATE=1` through the handoff command, keep the existing CLI package-update guard intact, and avoid creating an undeliverable restart continuation for unattended auto-update.

## AI assistance disclosure

I used AI assistance to inspect the issue, compare the prior closed PR, implement the patch, and run validation. I reviewed and understand the affected startup updater, managed-service handoff, restart scheduling, and CLI package-update guard paths.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: supervised gateway startup auto-update no longer runs the package update as a direct child of the live gateway service process with inherited service markers.

Real environment tested: local Linux user-systemd service using a packaged OpenClaw tarball from this PR, a throwaway npm prefix, isolated state/config, a unique profile-specific unit, a throwaway loopback port, and a throwaway token. The service started from package version 2026.6.1 and auto-updated through the beta channel to package version 2026.6.2-beta.1.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node .artifacts/issue-78492/run-systemd-package-proof.mjs
```

Evidence after fix:

Packaged service and update result:

```text
Unit: openclaw-gateway-proof784920adccb.service
Package before: 2026.6.1
Package after: 2026.6.2-beta.1

systemctl --user show:
MainPID=2744480
ExecMainCode=0
ExecMainStatus=0
Id=openclaw-gateway-proof784920adccb.service
ActiveState=active
SubState=running

managed handoff log:
[2026-06-04T18:06:36.961Z] starting managed update command: openclaw update --yes --channel beta --timeout 2700
[2026-06-04T18:06:36.963Z] managed update command pid=2744165
{
  "status": "ok",
  "mode": "npm",
  "root": "<proof-root>/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw",
  "before": { "version": "2026.6.1" },
  "after": { "version": "2026.6.2-beta.1" },
  "steps": [
    {
      "name": "global update",
      "command": "npm i -g --prefix <proof-root>/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/.openclaw-update-stage-... openclaw@beta --no-fund --no-audit --loglevel=error --min-release-age=0",
      "exitCode": 0
    },
    {
      "name": "global install swap",
      "command": "swap <proof-root>/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/.openclaw-update-stage-.../lib/node_modules/openclaw -> <proof-root>/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw",
      "exitCode": 0
    },
    {
      "name": "openclaw doctor",
      "command": "/usr/bin/node <proof-root>/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js doctor --non-interactive --fix",
      "exitCode": 0
    }
  ],
  "durationMs": 19607
}
[2026-06-04T18:07:09.355Z] managed update command exited code=0 signal=null
```

Gateway health after the package update and service restart:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "plugins": {
    "loaded": [
      "browser",
      "canvas",
      "device-pair",
      "file-transfer",
      "memory-core",
      "phone-control",
      "talk-voice"
    ],
    "errors": []
  },
  "sessions": {
    "path": "<proof-root>/state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json",
    "count": 0,
    "recent": []
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: a packaged user-systemd gateway started from this PR handed startup auto-update to the detached managed-service updater, ran the real `openclaw update --yes --channel beta --json` path through npm, swapped the package root, ran doctor successfully, restarted the gateway service, and returned healthy.

What was not tested: macOS launchd and Windows Scheduled Task proofs were not run. This proof covers the packaged Linux user-systemd path that ClawSweeper asked for; the existing focused tests still cover the helper-level channel, timeout, marker-stripping, and restart-continuation behavior.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ```
✓ without this patch, the gateway process printed an undeliverable 'gateway service restarted by auto-update' wall message and the supervisor saw a no-op restart that could stall further commands
```

## Tests and validation

```bash
pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test:fast  # all passing
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — unattended auto-update no longer produces an undeliverable restart continuation message, and the gateway process no longer runs the update command as a direct child with inherited service markers.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

What is the highest-risk area? The managed-service handoff path runs inside the live gateway service. If the handoff command fails or blocks, the gateway could stall or enter an inconsistent startup state.

How is that risk mitigated? The handoff uses the existing managed-service `spawnManagedService` helper with a timeout, same as `update.run`. The timeout prevents indefinite blocking, and the CLI package-update guard still applies on the handoff target.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper asked for packaged launchd or systemd proof; the updated proof above adds a real packaged Linux user-systemd auto-update run.

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archived transcripts from Dreaming session corpus
target.body:
## Summary

Archived session transcripts (`.jsonl.reset.*` and `.jsonl.deleted.*`) are
currently ingested into the Dreaming corpus, polluting analysis with
reset/deleted cron-spawned subagent noise. On a cron-heavy deployment,
this causes 67-91% of the daily corpus to be dominated by automation
noise, suppressing pattern discovery. This is particularly visible on
workspaces where cron jobs frequently spawn subagents whose transcripts get
reset/deleted but remain in the session directory as usage-counted archives.

## Changes

- Add `isSessionArchiveTranscriptPath` helper that detects `.jsonl.reset.*`
  and `.jsonl.deleted.*` archive transcript files
- Add archive-exclusion filter in `collectSessionIngestionBatches` so
  Dreaming corpus assembly skips archived transcripts
- Preserve `memory_search` archive indexing path unchanged (only Dreaming
  ingestion is affected)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Before the fix, `listSessionFilesForAgent` returns
archive transcript files (`.jsonl.reset.*`, `.jsonl.deleted.*`) that are ingested
into the Dreaming corpus. After the fix, the Dreaming corpus ingestion skips
these archive files while preserving the existing checkpoint-exclusion behavior.

Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, branch `fix/dreaming-exclude-archives`,
commit `713fe93a43`, using the project's test runner from a git worktree on latest main.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: All 45 existing dreaming-phases tests pass with no regressions.
```
✓ extension-memory extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts (45 tests) 3792ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  45 passed (45)
```

Archive regex verification:
```
session.jsonl.reset.2026-06-01T12-00-00.000Z → match (excluded)
session.jsonl.deleted.2026-05-31T23-59-59.999Z → match (excluded)
session.jsonl → no match (kept)
session-checkpoint.jsonl → no match (kept)
session.jsonl.bak.2026-06-01T12-00-00.000Z → no match (kept)
```

Observed result after fix: The Dreaming corpus ingestion loop now skips `.jsonl.reset.*`
and `.jsonl.deleted.*` archive transcript files. Primary session transcripts,
checkpoint transcripts (already excluded), and non-archive backup files are
unaffected. All existing tests pass.

What was not tested: End-to-end Dreaming memory quality on a live cron-heavy
workspace was not verified (requires a live deployment with multiple cron
jobs and subagents). The fix targets the source-level root cause confirmed
by ClawSweeper's code review.

## Test Plan

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 45 tests, all passing
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — 12 tests, all passing
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-yield.test.ts` — 1 test, passing
4. `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors

## Risk

- Low risk: the change is a purely additive filter in the Dreaming ingestion
  loop. Archive files are still indexed for `memory_search` through the
  unchanged `listSessionFilesForAgent` → `buildSessionEntry` path used by
  `manager-sync-ops.ts` and `qmd-manager.ts`.
- No change to the `memory_search` contract for reset/deleted archive
  indexing.

## Related Issue

Closes #90313
